name: Indiana SNAP Benefits
description: Indiana's SNAP program provides monthly food benefits on a Hoosier Works EBT card to help low-income individuals and families purchase groceries. Administered through the Family and Social Services Administration (FSSA).
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Indiana Medicaid (Healthy Indiana Plan - HIP)
description: Indiana's Medicaid program including the Healthy Indiana Plan (HIP) for adults. Provides health coverage including doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, mental health, dental, and vision services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant individuals, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: HIP adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 255% FPL

name: Indiana Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Benefits are calculated based on previous wages and can last up to 26 weeks.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own with sufficient earnings history
incomeLimitNotes: Weekly benefit based on previous wages; maximum weekly benefit applies

name: Indiana Housing and Community Development Authority (IHCDA)
description: Provides affordable housing programs including rental assistance, homebuyer assistance, homelessness prevention, and community development. Administers Housing Choice Vouchers and HOME funds.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income homebuyers and renters in Indiana
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally targets households below 80% of area median income

name: Indiana Legal Services
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Hoosiers across the state. Handles cases involving housing, family law, public benefits, consumer protection, and employment.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Indiana residents with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: WorkOne Indiana Career Centers
description: Indiana's network of workforce development centers providing free employment services including job search, career counseling, resume writing, skills assessments, and training opportunities.
eligibilitySummary: All Indiana job seekers and employers

name: Indiana LIHEAP - Energy Assistance Program (EAP)
description: Provides financial assistance to low-income households to help pay heating and cooling bills. Payments are made directly to energy providers on behalf of eligible households.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households at or below 150% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% FPL

name: Indiana 211
description: Free, confidential service connecting Hoosiers to local resources for food, housing, health care, utilities, and other essential services. Available 24/7 by phone and online.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Indiana seeking information about community resources
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Gleaners Food Bank of Indiana
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving 21 counties in central and southeastern Indiana. Distributes food through over 500 partner agencies including food pantries, shelters, and meal programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in central and southeastern Indiana

name: Community Harvest Food Bank of Northeast Indiana
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving northeast Indiana. Distributes food through partner agencies and mobile food distributions to individuals and families facing hunger.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in northeast Indiana

name: Feeding Indiana's Hungry (FIsH)
description: A statewide association of Feeding America food banks in Indiana coordinating hunger relief efforts across the state. Connects individuals to the nearest food bank and food assistance resources.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Indiana seeking food assistance

name: Indiana Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Serves Indiana veterans and their families with benefits counseling, claims assistance, employment support, and access to state veterans' homes and cemeteries.
eligibilitySummary: Indiana veterans and their dependents

name: Indiana Crisis and Suicide Hotline (Be Well Indiana)
description: Provides 24/7 crisis support and counseling for anyone experiencing mental health crisis, suicidal thoughts, or emotional distress. Connects callers with local crisis teams and resources.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Indiana experiencing a mental health or emotional crisis
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: Indiana Child Care Development Fund (CCDF)
description: Provides child care vouchers to help low-income families afford quality child care while parents work, attend school, or participate in job training. Covers licensed providers.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children under 13 who are working or in approved activities
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 127% FPL to enter; can continue up to 85% of state median income

name: Indiana TANF
description: Provides temporary cash assistance and support services to low-income families with children through the TANF program. Includes employment requirements and case management.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children under 18
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income limits; varies by family size

name: Indiana WIC Program
description: Provides nutritious foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals for pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level

name: Hoosier Healthwise (Indiana CHIP)
description: Indiana's Children's Health Insurance Program providing health coverage for uninsured children and pregnant women who are not eligible for traditional Medicaid.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 with family income up to 255% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Children with family income between 150-255% FPL

name: IndyGo - Indianapolis Public Transit
description: Operates bus service in the Indianapolis metropolitan area including the Red Line rapid transit. Offers reduced fare programs for seniors, people with disabilities, and Medicare card holders.
eligibilitySummary: Indianapolis area residents; reduced fares for seniors, disabled, and Medicare holders
hours: Service varies by route; customer service Mon-Fri 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM

name: Indianapolis Community Action of Central Indiana (CICOA)
description: Area agency on aging serving central Indiana providing services for seniors and people with disabilities including transportation, meals, caregiver support, and care coordination.
eligibilitySummary: Seniors 60+ and people with disabilities in central Indiana
incomeLimitNotes: Most services are free or based on ability to pay

name: Indiana Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes including insulation, air sealing, and heating system repairs to reduce energy bills.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters at or below 200% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Indiana Vocational Rehabilitation Services
description: Provides services to help people with disabilities prepare for, obtain, and maintain employment. Includes career counseling, training, job placement, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: 21st Century Scholars Program
description: Indiana's promise program providing up to four years of undergraduate tuition at eligible Indiana colleges and universities for income-eligible students who enroll in 7th or 8th grade and complete pledge requirements.
eligibilitySummary: Indiana students who enroll in 7th/8th grade and meet income eligibility
incomeLimitNotes: Must be eligible for free or reduced-price lunch at time of enrollment

name: Indiana Adult Education
description: Provides free classes for adults including GED preparation, English language learning, basic literacy, numeracy, and workforce readiness at locations across Indiana.
eligibilitySummary: Adults 18+ without a high school diploma or needing English language skills

name: 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline - Indiana
description: Provides free, confidential support for anyone in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. Call or text 988 to connect with a trained counselor 24/7.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing suicidal thoughts, mental health crisis, or emotional distress
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in 150+ languages

name: Indiana Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Provides crisis support, safety planning, and referrals to shelters and services for survivors of domestic violence. Operated by the Indiana Coalition Against Domestic Violence.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence, dating violence, or sexual assault
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: Fort Wayne Community Action (CANI Northeast Indiana)
description: Community action agency serving northeast Indiana providing energy assistance, emergency services, Head Start, weatherization, and self-sufficiency programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in northeast Indiana

name: Indiana Rural Transit (INDOT Public Transit)
description: Coordinates public transit services across rural Indiana through regional transit providers. Provides demand-response transportation for seniors, people with disabilities, and the general public.
eligibilitySummary: Indiana residents in rural areas; priority for seniors and people with disabilities

name: Indiana Disability Rights
description: Provides free legal advocacy and information to people with disabilities in Indiana. Handles issues including abuse and neglect, discrimination, education, employment, and access to services.
eligibilitySummary: People with disabilities in Indiana

name: South Bend / St. Joseph County Housing Authority
description: Provides affordable housing options and Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers for low-income families in the South Bend and St. Joseph County area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in South Bend and St. Joseph County
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 50% of area median income for Section 8
